subject: Improve clock transition stability and get OSC1 freq from DT (#35)
author: Michael Huang

impact.effect_if_absent: CPU frequency scaling (cpufreq) subsystem would be non-functional; users cannot scale CPU frequency from 400 MHz to 1.2 GHz; no build or boot error would result—the feature silently fails to register

license_provenance: GPL-2.0 (SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0 in socfpga-cpufreq.c); author: Michael Huang <coolbho3000@gmail.com>
notes: This commit (#35) is squashed together with commit #34 (Add cpufreq/overclock driver) in carried patch 0003. It improves the initial cpufreq driver by (1) removing the hardcoded 25 MHz OSC1 constant and reading it from the device tree via of_get_property() instead, and (2) adding PLL bypass/unbypass sequencing in set_dividers() for more stable clock transitions. The commit modifies calculate_vco_clock_hz() to use a static variable socfpga_cpufreq_osc1_hz instead of the hardcoded #define. At init time (socfpga_cpufreq_init()), it traverses /clkmgr@ffd04000/clocks/osc1 and reads the clock-frequency property from DT, converting it from big-endian. Verified against vanilla 6.18.38: no socfpga-cpufreq.c exists in vanilla (grep -rin 'socfpga|altera|altr' drivers/cpufreq/ returns no matches). Not upstream; never submitted to linux-pm. Forward-ported successfully from 5.15 to 6.18 as part of patch 0003. Known latent bug deliberately preserved for hardware safety: wait_for_fsm() calls wait_on_bit() with CLKMGR_STAT_BUSY (a mask, BIT(0)==1) where a bit *number* is expected, so it polls bit 1 instead of bit 0. This is intentional to avoid changing PLL transition timing without DE10-Nano hardware validation. In practice the bug is benign because PLL settling is covered by register-write latency. **CRITICAL: Silent failure mode.** Dropping this commit causes no build/boot error; cpufreq simply fails to register and users lose frequency-scaling capability. Userspace consumers: community overclock scripts on /media/fat that write to scaling_max_freq. Main_MiSTer itself does not reference cpufreq (verified in provenance-doc). Configuration: CONFIG_ARM_SOCFPGA_CPUFREQ=y in linux.config. Fully agrees with provenance-doc sections 719-874.
